subject: UX & Research
title: Recognition vs. Recall
description: A quick win: understand Recognition vs. Recall.

hook.text: Which is easier: remembering a password or picking your face from a photo lineup? Recognition wins every time.

buildup.text: Recall forces the brain to retrieve from scratch. Recognition just needs a match against what's shown.

discovery.text: This is why dropdown menus beat blank text fields. Users recognize options faster than they recall them.

twist.text: Command-line interfaces demand recall. GUIs offer recognition. That's why GUIs won the mass market.

climax.text: Nielsen's heuristic #6: minimize the user's memory load. Show options, don't force users to recall them.

punchline.text: Show, don't ask. Recognition beats recall every time.

quiz.question: Why are dropdown menus easier to use than blank text fields?
options: Users recognize options faster than they recall them from memory
Dropdowns load faster than text fields
